description:
GOAL: The "gift" mechanism. When the runtime can prove sender owns the entire reachability of a value, transfer arena ownership to receiver. O(1) instead of deep copy.

## Decisions captured (from design discussion)

- Closes BEAM's biggest performance hole (message-copy cost).
- Single shared address space + per-process heap means: pointers across processes ARE valid; the constraint is GC ownership, not addressability.
- Static path: linear/uniqueness types in the typer (separate ticket arc, not blocking this); arena-tagged allocation when typer can prove uniqueness.
- Dynamic path: at send time, walk msg's closure-of-reachability. If every reachable object is in (the same arena ∪ shared region), transfer arena ownership. Else fall back to copy.
- Fallback: if not transferable, copy (uses .19.3's deep-copy walker). Always correct.

## Heap structure (from design discussion)

- Per-process MAIN heap (the default, traced by sender's GC).
- TRANSFERABLE ARENAS: small mmap'd regions, internally pointer-closed (refs only inside same arena or shared region — never into a process-private main heap). Owned by exactly one process at a time. Refcount-managed across processes.
- SHARED globally-immutable region: atom table (already needed), code, persistent literals. Refcounted at region granularity.

## Send fast path

```
send(pid, x):
  if x is in a transferable arena owned by sender AND x is the arena's root:
    transfer arena ownership to receiver  // O(1)
  else:
    walk reachability of x
    if closure-of-reachable points only to (x's reachability ∪ shared region):
      promote: alloc fresh arena, copy reachability into it, transfer  // O(size), no double-copy
    else:
      copy into receiver's heap (deep-copy from .19.3)  // O(size)
```

## Deliverables

- Arena allocator: separate from main heap. Each arena is a small mmap'd region with its own bump pointer + GC mark bits.
- Arena registry: tracks which process currently owns each arena.
- Allocator API: alloc_in_arena(size, arena_handle) for opt-in arena allocation.
- Send fast path (described above).
- Promote step: walk + alloc-into-fresh-arena.
- Closed-arena invariant maintenance: when arena pointer P is allocated, runtime checks pointers stored into P's objects MUST point to other arena objects or shared. Enforced via write-barrier-on-arena-store (cheap with strict immutability — we never re-write fields).

## Tests

- Allocate value in arena via opt-in API; send to receiver; verify O(1) transfer (e.g., timing assertion or invariant check).
- Send a value NOT in arena — verify copy fallback works.
- After transfer, sender can no longer access the arena (arena GC tracks owner).
- Runtime walk-and-decide: a tuple-of-ints with no inbound refs gets promoted; same tuple with a captured closure into main heap falls back to copy.

## Risks / open

- Closed-arena invariant maintenance. With strict immutability we never modify fields after construction — so invariant is established once at alloc time. If we ever add mutation (vec builders, accumulators), we need write barriers per arena.
- Static `unique[T]` typer support: deferred. Runtime walk-and-decide is always correct; static path is performance-only.
- Performance of walk: same cost as copy for the unhappy path. Static path matters a lot for measurable improvement.

## Out of scope

- Static uniqueness types in typer (follow-up arc — could be its own epic).
- Per-arena write barriers (only needed when mutation lands).
- Arena-aware data structures (e.g. ListBuilder that writes directly into arena) — follow-up.
title: Transferable arenas + zero-copy send fast path
